column3: This downtown **chophouse** serves dry-aged steaks and classic cocktails.
We stopped at the local **alehouse** for a pint after work.
The new **taphouse** offers over thirty rotating craft beers.
A hidden **speakeasy** behind the bookshelf served prohibition-era cocktails.
We stopped at a Texas **roadhouse** for ribs and cold beer.
Their lunch **carryout** is ready in under ten minutes.
Let's grab Chinese **takeaway** and eat at home tonight.
The Turkish **meze** platter featured hummus and stuffed grape leaves.
Our **antipasto** plate had cured meats and marinated olives.
The tuna **sashimi** melted on my tongue like butter.
Shrimp **tempura** should be crunchy on the outside and juicy inside.
We dipped bread into the bubbling cheese **fondue**.
Homemade potato **gnocchi** came in a light sage butter sauce.
The mushroom **risotto** was creamy and perfectly seasoned.
Louisiana **gumbo** combines seafood with okra and smoked sausage.
Each pork **dumpling** was steamed in a bamboo basket.
The **panfried** sea bass had a golden crispy skin.
The **chargrilled** octopus tasted wonderfully smoky and perfectly tender.
The **broiled** lobster tail came with drawn butter.
The sauce **simmered** gently on the back burner for an hour.
Briefly **blanched** asparagus stayed bright green and crisp.
The **marinated** chicken soaked in yogurt and spices overnight.
Freshly **whipped** cream crowned the delicate strawberry shortcake dessert.
Creamy garlic **mashed** potatoes accompanied the tender roast beef.
Every bite of the **succulent** lamb was rich and tender.
Each course was beautifully **plated** with microgreens and edible flowers.
The cocktail came **garnished** with a twist of lemon peel.
Warm honey was **drizzled** across the goat cheese salad.
The bread tasted **stale**, so we asked for a fresh basket.
This **savory** tart combined goat cheese and caramelized onions.
The broth was deeply **flavorful** from hours of slow cooking.
Every dish on the tasting menu was absolutely **exquisite**.
The dark chocolate mousse was truly **delectable** and unforgettable.
The apple pie was **scrumptious** with vanilla ice cream.
The **mouthwatering** aroma of fresh pizza filled the room.
The burnt fish was completely **inedible** and we sent it back.
The watery soup was **flavorless** and deeply disappointing for us.
The pasta tasted **reheated** rather than freshly prepared.
The venison had a strong **gamey** taste I enjoyed.
The **smoky** flavor of the barbecue ribs was irresistible.
The arugula salad had a pleasantly **peppery** bite.
This aged cheddar has a wonderfully **nutty** flavor.
The salmon tasted too **fishy** and was probably not fresh.
A **zesty** lime dressing brightened the whole salad.
The **tangy** barbecue sauce complemented the smoked brisket.
The lemon **tart** flavor made my mouth pucker pleasantly.
Fresh oysters have a pleasant **briny** ocean flavor.
This dark ale has a rich **malty** finish.
The yolk of a poached egg should stay nicely **runny**.
The fries were overly **oily** and left grease on my napkin.
The finely **marbled** wagyu beef was incredibly tender and rich.
Potatoes and rice are considered **starchy** side dishes.
The **chunky** tomato sauce had big pieces of vegetables.
This **hearty** beef stew is perfect for a cold evening.
That bowl of ramen was incredibly **satisfying** after a long day.
Grilled salmon makes a **nutritious** and delicious main course.
Can I **substitute** fries with a green salad please.
Guests can **customize** their pizza with any toppings they like.
Feel free to **modify** the dish if you have any allergies.
The restaurant charges a ten dollar **corkage** fee for outside wine.
Our server was incredibly **attentive** throughout the whole dinner.
The hostess greeted every guest with a **polite** smile.
The waiter was very **helpful** in explaining the menu options.
The sommelier remained **courteous** even when we hesitated for long.
The **unhelpful** server rolled his eyes when we asked about ingredients.
The side salad looked **wilted** and clearly wasn't fresh.
My latte arrived **tepid** instead of steaming hot.
Marseille is famous for its classic fish **bouillabaisse**.
Provencal **ratatouille** combines tomatoes with zucchini and eggplant.
The **bruschetta** was topped with diced tomatoes and basil.
Beef **carpaccio** consists of thinly sliced raw meat.
Hungarian **goulash** is a rich beef stew with paprika.
The **tiramisu** was layered with coffee-soaked ladyfingers and mascarpone.
